UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile information, including personal details and contact information.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

name.description: The full name of the user.

age.description: The age of the user in years.

phone.description: The contact phone number of the user.

EmergencyContact.title: EmergencyContact
EmergencyContact.description: Represents an emergency contact for a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the EmergencyContact entity.

name.description: The full name of the emergency contact.

phone.description: The contact phone number of the emergency contact.

relation.description: The relationship of the emergency contact to the user (e.g., 'parent', 'sibling', 'friend').

MedicalHistoryEntry.title: MedicalHistoryEntry
MedicalHistoryEntry.description: Represents a single entry in a user's medical history.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MedicalHistoryEntry entity.

date.description: The date of the medical entry.

symptoms.description: A description of the symptoms experienced during this medical event.

diagnosis.description: The medical diagnosis made for this entry.

prescription.description: Details of the prescribed medication or treatment for this entry.

Doctor.title: Doctor
Doctor.description: Represents a medical doctor available on the platform.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Doctor entity.

name.description: The full name of the doctor.

specialty.description: The medical specialty of the doctor (e.g., 'Cardiologist', 'Pediatrician').

hospitalId.description: Reference to the Hospital where the doctor primarily practices. (Relationship: Hospital 1:N Doctor)

rating.description: The average rating of the doctor, typically on a scale (e.g., out of 5).

fee.description: The consultation fee charged by the doctor.

availability.description: An array of available time slots or dates for appointments (e.g., '2024-07-20T09:00:00Z', 'Monday 09:00-12:00').

locationLatitude.description: The geographical latitude coordinate of the doctor's practice location.

locationLongitude.description: The geographical longitude coordinate of the doctor's practice location.

Hospital.title: Hospital
Hospital.description: Represents a healthcare facility with real-time bed tracking capabilities.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Hospital entity.

bedsAvailable.description: The current number of general hospital beds available.

icuBeds.description: The current number of ICU (Intensive Care Unit) beds available.

ventilatorCount.description: The current number of ventilators available.

locationLatitude.description: The geographical latitude coordinate of the hospital.

locationLongitude.description: The geographical longitude coordinate of the hospital.

Appointment.title: Appointment
Appointment.description: Represents a scheduled appointment between a user and a doctor at a hospital.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Appointment entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who booked the appointment. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Appointment)

doctorId.description: Reference to the Doctor for this appointment. (Relationship: Doctor 1:N Appointment)

hospitalId.description: Reference to the Hospital where the appointment will take place. (Relationship: Hospital 1:N Appointment)

dateTime.description: The scheduled date and time of the appointment.

status.description: The current status of the appointment (e.g., 'booked', 'confirmed', 'completed', 'cancelled').

BloodDonor.title: BloodDonor
BloodDonor.description: Represents an individual registered as a blood donor.

id.description: Unique identifier for the BloodDonor entity.

name.description: The full name of the blood donor.

bloodGroup.description: The blood group of the donor (e.g., 'A+', 'O-', 'AB+').

locationLatitude.description: The geographical latitude coordinate of the donor's current or preferred donation location.

locationLongitude.description: The geographical longitude coordinate of the donor's current or preferred donation location.

phone.description: The contact phone number of the blood donor.

availability.description: An array of available time slots or dates for blood donation (e.g., '2024-07-25', 'Weekends').

definition.description: Stores a user's profile information, including embedded arrays for emergency contacts and medical history, and an array of references to their appointments. Authorization is path-based: `request.auth.uid == userId`.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, corresponding to Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Stores information about a medical doctor available on the platform. This collection is publicly readable, with write access typically restricted to administrators. No hierarchical authorization dependencies.

params.name: doctorId
params.description: The unique identifier for the doctor.

definition.description: Stores information about healthcare facilities, including real-time bed tracking. This collection is publicly readable, with write access typically restricted to administrators. No hierarchical authorization dependencies.

params.name: hospitalId
params.description: The unique identifier for the hospital.

definition.description: Stores details of a scheduled appointment. Includes denormalized 'userId', 'doctorId', and 'hospitalId' for efficient authorization and querying without hierarchical dependencies. Access is granted to the associated user and doctor.

params.name: appointmentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the appointment.

definition.description: Stores information about individuals registered as blood donors. This collection is publicly readable, with write access typically restricted to administrators or the donor themselves. No hierarchical authorization dependencies.

params.name: donorId
params.description: The unique identifier for the blood donor.

definition.description: Used for Database Access Control (DBAC). The existence of a document at this path signifies that the corresponding 'userId' has administrator privileges. Authorization is independent, relying solely on `exists(/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid))`.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who holds an administrator role.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure for MedConnect+ prioritizes security, scalability, and debuggability by adhering to the core design principles and specific mandates, while strictly implementing the provided 'Exact Schema' for document structure. 

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):**

1.  **User-specific data (`/users/{userId}`):** All private user data, including their `UserProfile`, embedded `emergencyContacts`, and embedded `medicalHistory` entries, are stored within the `/users/{userId}` document. Authorization for this document and its contents is strictly path-based: `request.auth.uid == userId`. This makes the `UserProfile` document self-contained for authorization purposes, eliminating the need for `get()` calls to a parent document. Since emergency contacts and medical history entries are embedded arrays, their access rights are directly inherited from the parent `UserProfile` document, simplifying rules. The `appointments` field, storing an array of references, is also protected by the same user-specific rule.
2.  **Appointments (`/appointments/{appointmentId}`):** The `Appointment` document explicitly includes `userId`, `doctorId`, and `hospitalId` fields. This denormalization is crucial for Authorization Independence. Security rules can directly check `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId` (for the user) or `request.auth.uid == resource.data.doctorId` (for the doctor) without performing any cross-collection `get()` operations. This ensures atomic operations and simplifies rule debugging.
3.  **Public/Global Data (`/doctors/{doctorId}`, `/hospitals/{hospitalId}`, `/bloodDonors/{donorId}`):** These collections store entities that are generally publicly readable but may have restricted write access (e.g., for administrators). Their security posture is self-contained, requiring no dependencies on other documents for authorization.
4.  **Admin Roles (`/roles_admin/{userId}`):** This collection uses the DBAC (Database Access Control) pattern. The mere existence of a document at `/roles_admin/{request.auth.uid}` grants admin privileges. This is an authorization-independent check, using `exists()`, but on a dedicated collection specifically for roles, not on data collections.

**QAPs (Queries Are Not Filters) Support:**

1.  **User-specific data (`/users/{userId}`):** A user can only `get` or `list` their own `/users/{userId}` document. This inherently enforces that any query for user profile data is already filtered by the authenticated user's UID by virtue of the path structure.
2.  **Appointments (`/appointments/{appointmentId}`):** For a user to retrieve their appointments, their client-side query *must* include a `where('userId', '==', request.auth.uid)` clause. Similarly, for a doctor, a `where('doctorId', '==', request.auth.uid)` clause would be required. Firestore security rules will enforce these query filters, preventing unauthorized access or accidental exposure of other users' or doctors' appointments. The denormalized `userId` and `doctorId` fields facilitate these secure `list` operations.
3.  **Public/Global Data (`/doctors`, `/hospitals`, `/bloodDonors`):** These collections are intended for general public lookup (e.g., 'Cardiologist near me', 'Hospitals with beds'). As they are publicly readable, `list` operations are inherently secure and do not require additional filtering by security rules based on the requesting user's identity (though client-side filters can still be applied for display purposes).
